focus: Reduction & Weak Forms — Advanced Function-Word Compression
focus_ar: التخفيض والصيغ الضعيفة — ضغط متقدم للكلمات الوظيفية
arabic_challenge: L1 taught you that function words reduce to schwa. L2's Reduction & Weak Forms goes further: entire function words (and, to, of, for, have, has) develop specific, predictable REDUCED SPELLINGS in fast native speech that go beyond a simple schwa vowel change — they lose whole sounds. Arabic speakers who've mastered schwa still often keep every consonant in these words, which is the next barrier to sounding fully native.

isolation.instructions: In fast connected speech, 'and' frequently loses its final /d/ entirely and reduces to just /ən/ or even a bare /n/ sound attached to the surrounding words.
isolation.instructions_ar: في الكلام السريع المتصل، and غالبًا بتخسر صوت /d/ في الآخر تمامًا وبتختصر لـ /ən/ أو حتى مجرد /n/ ملتصقة بالكلام المحيط.

sentence_practice: I like fish and chips with salt and pepper.
We danced to rock and roll, up and down all night.
Bread and butter, salt and pepper — simple and good.
daily_drills.record_this: I like fish and chips with salt and pepper — we danced to rock and roll, up and down, with bread and butter after.
daily_drills.self_check: Did 'and' shrink to a light /ən/ or /n/ throughout, rather than a full, clear 'and'?

isolation.instructions: Building on L1's dropped-H pattern, the auxiliary 'have' often reduces further to just /əv/ (sounding like 'of'), and 'has' to /əz/, in fast native speech.
isolation.instructions_ar: بناء على نمط حذف H من المستوى الأول، فعل المساعدة have غالبًا بيختصر أكتر لـ /əv/ (بيبان كإنه of)، وhas لـ /əz/، في الكلام السريع.
practice: I could have gone → I could-əv gone
she should have known → she should-əv known

sentence_practice: I could have gone, but I should have called first.
She would have known if she must have asked.
We might have missed it — you should have told me.
daily_drills.record_this: I could have gone, but I should have called first — she would have known if she must have asked, and we might have missed it.
daily_drills.self_check: Did 'have' after modal verbs (could/should/would/might/must) reduce to a quick /əv/ sound, almost like 'of'?

isolation.instructions: Building on L1's schwa work, 'to' and 'for' compress even further in truly fast native speech, almost disappearing into the surrounding words.
isolation.instructions_ar: بناء على شغل الشوا من المستوى الأول، to و for بتنضغط أكتر في الكلام الطبيعي السريع جدًا، وبتكاد تختفي وسط الكلمات المحيطة.
practice: going to → gonna (in very casual speech)
want to → wanna (in very casual speech)

sentence_practice: I'm going to the store — do you want to come?
I've been waiting for you and looking for my keys.
I have to go, but I want to stay a bit longer.
daily_drills.record_this: I'm going to the store — do you want to come? I've been waiting for you and looking for my keys, but I have to go soon.
daily_drills.self_check: Note: 'gonna'/'wanna' are very casual — appropriate for informal conversation, not formal presentations. Can you distinguish when each register is appropriate?

target_sounds: reduction chains: multiple weak forms in one phrase

isolation.instructions: Real native speech often stacks several reduced forms in a row. Practice recognizing and producing these reduction chains.
isolation.instructions_ar: الكلام الطبيعي الحقيقي غالبًا بيجمع أكتر من صيغة مخففة ورا بعض. تدرب على تمييز وإنتاج سلاسل التخفيض دي.
practice: I should have gone to the store for some milk and bread → multiple reductions stacked

sentence_practice: I should have gone to the store for some milk and bread.
I've been waiting for you and looking for my keys and my phone.
We have to go and get some fish and chips for dinner.
daily_drills.record_this: I should have gone to the store for some milk and bread — I've been waiting for you and looking for my keys and my phone, so we have to go and get some fish and chips for dinner.
daily_drills.self_check: How many separate reduced forms can you identify in this one sentence? Aim for at least 5.

target_sounds: reduction at natural, unrehearsed speaking speed

isolation.instructions: Test whether these advanced reductions survive when you're speaking without a script, under the pressure of thinking of your own words.
isolation.instructions_ar: اختبر لو التخفيضات المتقدمة دي بتفضل موجودة وانت بتتكلم بدون نص، تحت ضغط التفكير في كلماتك الخاصة.
practice: Speak spontaneously for 30 seconds about your weekend plans, using natural reductions.

word_practice: (free choice based on your own weekend plans)
daily_drills.record_this: (Free speech: describe your weekend plans for 30 seconds, using natural reduced forms where appropriate.)
daily_drills.self_check: Listen back — did reductions appear naturally without you planning them, or only when you consciously tried?

daily_drills.target_sounds: Review: 'and' reduction, 'have/has' reduction, 'to/for' reduction, reduction chains, spontaneous speech
challenge_sentences: I should have gone to the store for some fish and chips, and I would have if I hadn't been waiting for you.
We're going to need some bread and butter — do you want to come and get some for us?
I could have told you, but I was looking for my keys and waiting for the bus.
daily_drills.record_this: I should have gone to the store for some fish and chips, and I would have if I hadn't been waiting for you — we're going to need some bread and butter, so do you want to come and get some?
daily_drills.self_check: Mark every reduced function word in this passage. How many distinct reduction types (and/have/to/for) appear?

test_yourself.instructions: Record this passage naturally, applying advanced reduction patterns throughout.
test_yourself.passage: I should have gone to the store yesterday for some fish and chips, but I was waiting for a call and looking for my keys. My friend and I were going to meet for coffee, and we would have talked about bread and butter recipes, but I must have forgotten the time. I have to go and get milk and eggs now — do you want to come with me?

sounds_to_check.'and' reduction: fish and chips, my friend and I, bread and butter, milk and eggs
sounds_to_check.'have/has' reduction (modal + have): should have, would have, must have
sounds_to_check.'to/for' reduction: going to, waiting for, looking for, have to

scoring_guide.90_100: All reduction types applied naturally and consistently at conversational speed.
scoring_guide.70_89: Most reductions present; a few full pronunciations under stress.
scoring_guide.50_69: Reductions present in drilled phrases only, inconsistent in new combinations.
scoring_guide.below_50: Full, careful pronunciation throughout — go back and repeat Days 1-4.

for_arabic_speakers: This week directly extends L1's schwa and dropped-H work — think of it as those same principles applied even more aggressively to a specific set of extremely common function words.
'Gonna' and 'wanna' are correct and natural in casual speech, but should NOT be used in formal presentations, essays, or the advancement exam's formal sections — know when each register applies.
This is Blueprint §5.3 Rule 4 (Reduction & Weak Forms), explicitly assigned to L2 Week 2 — the final piece of the connected-speech linking framework started in L1.
supplementary_notes.recording_requirement: Record the 'record_this' sentence every day. By Day 7, compare your fluency here to your L1 Week 8 (schwa) recording — this should sound like a natural evolution of that skill.
